description.en: Preserving and sharing the rich cultural heritage of the Afar people
description.am: የአፋር ህዝብ የበለጸገ ባህላዊ ቅርስ መጠበቅ እና ማካፈል

hero.title: Discover the Rich Heritage of the Afar People
hero.subtitle: Explore the ancient traditions, customary laws, and nomadic lifestyle of one of the Horn of Africa's most resilient cultures

about.title: The Afar People: Guardians of the Danakil
about.content: The Afar people, also known as the Danakil, are a Cushitic ethnic group inhabiting the Horn of Africa. Primarily residing in the Afar Region of Ethiopia, northern Djibouti, and southern Eritrea, they are renowned for their resilience in one of the hottest and most inhospitable environments on Earth - the Danakil Depression.

features.title: Traditional Culture
features.description: Discover the unique customs, ceremonies, and social structures that define Afar identity

features.title: Customary Law (Mada'a)
features.description: Understand the traditional legal system that has governed Afar society for generations

features.title: Pastoral Lifestyle
features.description: Explore the nomadic traditions and the deep connection between Afar people and their land

hero.title: Qafar Qaada Dacayro Baxxaqis
hero.subtitle: Qafar qaada, mada'aay, kee manol xayloh tan gexsititte qambal

hero.title: የአፋር ህዝብ የበለጸገ ቅርስ ይወቁ
hero.subtitle: በአፍሪካ ቀንድ ከሚገኙት በጣም ጠንካራ ባህሎች መካከል አንዱ የሆነውን ጥንታዊ ወጎችን፣ ልማዳዊ ህጎችን እና ዘላን አኗኗርን ይመርምሩ

sections.title: Social Structure
sections.content: Afar society is organized into clans and sub-clans, with a strong emphasis on kinship and collective responsibility.

sections.title: Traditional Attire
sections.content: The distinctive Sanafil (waistcloth) and Shash (headscarf) for men, and the colorful Gambo (dress) for women.

sections.title: The Jile Dagger
sections.content: The Jile is a traditional curved dagger that symbolizes honor, bravery, and manhood in Afar culture.

en.title: Mada'a - Afar Customary Law
en.content: The Mada'a is the traditional legal system of the Afar people, governing everything from marriage and inheritance to conflict resolution and resource management.

en.title: Pastoral Lifestyle
en.content: The Afar are traditionally pastoralists, herding camels, goats, sheep, and cattle across the arid landscapes of the Horn of Africa.
